ItemGroupDef.Name: TA
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per planned Element per Arm

ItemGroupDef.Name: TE
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per planned Element

ItemGroupDef.Name: TI
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per I/E crierion

ItemGroupDef.Name: TS
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per trial summary parameter value

ItemGroupDef.Name: TV
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per planned Visit per Arm

ItemGroupDef.Name: DM
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SE
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per actual Element per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SV
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per subject per actual visit

ItemGroupDef.Name: CM
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per recorded intervention occurrence or constant-dosing interval per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: EC
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per protocol-specified study treatment, collected-dosing interval, per subject, per mood

ItemGroupDef.Name: EX
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per protocol-specified study treatment, constant-dosing interval, per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SU
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per substance type per reported occurrence per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: AE
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per adverse event per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: DS
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per disposition status or protocol milestone per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: DV
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per protocol deviation per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: MH
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per medical history event per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: EG
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per ECG observation per replicate per time point or one record per ECG observation per beat per visit per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: FA
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per finding, per object, per time point, per visit per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: IE
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per inclusion/exclusion criterion not met per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: IS
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per test per visit per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: LB
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per lab test per time point per visit per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: PC
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per sample characteristic or time-point concentration per reference time point or per analyte per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: PE
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per body system or abnormality per visit per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: PP
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per PK parameter per time-concentration profile per modeling method per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: VS
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per vital sign measurement per time point per visit per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPAE
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPCM
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPDM
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPDS
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPDV
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPEG
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPIS
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPLB
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPPC
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPPE
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

ItemGroupDef.Name: SUPPVS
ItemGroupDef.def:Structure: One record per IDVAR, IDVARVAL, and QNAM value per subject

Description.TranslatedText: Planned Order of Element within Arm
Planned Order of Element within Arm
Study Day of Start of Observation
Study Day of End of Observation
Study Day of Start of Visit
Study Day of End of Visit
Reported Name of Drug, Med, or Therapy
Study Day of Start of Medication
Study Day of End of Medication
End Relative to Reference Time Point
Study Day of Start of Treatment
Study Day of End of Treatment
Study Day of Start of Treatment
Study Day of End of Treatment
Reported Term for the Adverse Event
Body System or Organ Class Code
Study Day of Start of Adverse Event
Study Day of End of Adverse Event
Reported Term for the Disposition Event
Study Day of Start of Disposition Event
Study Day of Start of Deviation
Reported Term for the Medical History
Start Date/Time of Medical History Event
End Relative to Reference Time Point
ECG Test or Examination Short Name
Result or Finding in Original Units
Result or Finding in Original Units
I/E Criterion Result in Std Format
Results or Findings in Original Units
Result or Finding in Standard Format
Lab Test or Examination Short Name
Result or Finding in Original Units
Reference Range Lower Limit in Orig Unit
Reference Range Upper Limit in Orig Unit
Reference Range for Char Rslt-Std Units
Result or Finding in Original Units
Result or Finding in Standard Format
Actual Study Day of Specimen Collection
Study Day of End of Observation
Result or Finding in Standard Format
Result or Finding in Original Units
Result or Finding in Standard Format
Result or Finding in Original Units
History or evidence of clinically relevant pathology related to cardiovascular (CVS), central nervous system (CNS), respiratory tract, gastrointestinal (GI), endocrinology, immunology, hematology,
Presence of erythema, edema, inflammation, damage or other condition at the application site that would affect drug absorption;
Presence of skin disorders, tattoos, or other skin conditions that may interfere with the objectives of the study (as per investigator evaluation);
Consumed more than 14 units of alcohol per week at any time within the 3 months before screening or reluctant to avoid alcohol intake during the duration of the trial;
Any subject who consumes more than 5 cigarettes per day within 3 months prior to screening, or who cannot stop using any tobacco- or nicotine-containing product during the duration of the trial;
Systemic or topical use of prescription drug within 14 days before study drug dosing, including nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, sucralfate or herbal preparations. Use of an over-the-counter
Any clinically significant result in the screening evaluations of Hepatitis B surface antigen, Hepatitis C antibody, human immunodeficiency virus antibody/antigen, and treponema pallidum antibody;
Any subject who is unwilling to stay in the clinical center for the required duration as per the protocol or to consume the standard meal to be provided as per the protocol;
Any subject who has an acute or significant disease within 7 days prior to study drug administration or has a hospitalization within 1 month prior to study drug administration;
Any other situation which, in the opinion of the Investigator, would prevent the subject from fully participating in the study.
Positive screening on the alcohol (breathalyzer) test (> 0.0 mg/100 mL) or the urine drugs of abuse test (morphine, methamphetamine, ketamine, ecstasy, and marijuana);
Weight: male >= 50.0 kg, female >= 45.0 kg; with calculated body mass index (BMI) between 19.0 and 26.0 kg/m2 (both inclusive), BMI = weight (kg) / height2 (m2);
All values of vital signs, physical examination, laboratory tests, and 12-lead ECG are within allowed normal limits or not clinically significant. Results of pregnancy test are negative;
Subject withdrew due to the AE
Whether with the scheduled sampling time

EnumeratedItem.CodedValue: HIV Antigen and Antibody
Hepatitis B Virus Surface Antigen
Hepatitis B Virus e Antibody
Hepatitis C Virus Antibody
Treponema pallidum Antibody

BMI does not satisfy inclusion criteria, and the abnormalities in serum biochemistry and urinalysis are judged as clinically significant.
BMI does not satisfy inclusion criteria.
Damage in the skin of foot led to change of the repair period, which may interfere with absorption of drug.
Diagnosed with eczema by another hospital, which may interfere with the study as per investigator evaluation.
ENTERED INTO TRIAL
High blood pressure.
History of urticarial, which may interfere with the study.
INFORMED CONSENT OBTAINED
In the opinion of the investigator, the subject has difficulty in donating blood and is not suitable for the trial.
In the opinion of the investigator, there was prior medical history or evidence that would interfere with the outcome of study.
No vacancy.
Not included due to personal reasons.
Other reasons
Presence of tattoos on skin that may interfere with the objectives of the study as per investigator evaluation.
Presence of damage in the skin of foot which does not satisfy the requirements of protocol.
Presence of damage in the skin of foot.
Presence of skin conditions that may interfere with absorption of drug as per investigator evaluation.
Presence of skin conditions that may interfere with the objectives of the study as per investigator evaluation.
Subjects had symptoms of cold within 1 week prior to the visit.
The abnormalities in hematology and treponema pallidum antibody are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in hematology and serum biochemistry are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in hematology and urinalysis are judged as clinically significant, and presence of damage in the skin of foot does not satisfy the requirements of protocol.
The abnormalities in hematology and urinalysis are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in hematology, urinalysis and human immunodeficiency virus antibody/antigen are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in hematology, urinalysis, serum biochemistry and serology are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in physical examination and hematology are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in physical examination and urinalysis are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in physical examination, hematology and serum biochemistry are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in urinalysis and physical examination are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in urinalysis and pregnancy test are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in urinalysis and serology are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormalities in urinalysis and serum biochemistry are judged as clinically significant.
The abnormality in 12-lead electrocardiogram is judged as clinically significant.
The abnormality in hematology is judged as clinically significant.
The abnormality in hepatatis B virology is judged as clinically significant.
The abnormality in physical examination is judged as clinically significant.
The abnormality in serology is judged as clinically significant.
The abnormality in serum biochemistry is judged as clinically significant.
The abnormality in urinalysis is judged as clinically significant.
The abnormality in urinalysis is judged as clinically significant，and presence of damage in the skin of foot.
The abnormality in vital signs is judged as clinically significant at admission to the clinic.
The abnormality in vital signs is judged as clinically significant.
The amount of alcohol subject consumed per week does not satisfy the requirements of protocol.
The amount of cigarettes subject consumed does not satisfy the requirements of protocol.
The amount of cigarettes subject consumed per day does not satisfy the requirements of protocol.
The condition of the top of right foot is not suitable for the trial.
The result of urine drugs of abuse test is positive.
The subject consumed more than 10 cigarettes per day.
The subject consumed more than 5 cigarettes per day within 3 months prior to screening.
The subject is unwilling to consume the standard meal to be provided as per the protocol.
The subject was unfit to participate in the study in the opinion of the investigator.
Use of many kinds of drug within 14 days before study.
Use of vitamin C and fish liver oil within 7 days before study drug dosing;
Weight and BMI does not satisfy inclusion criteria.
Weight does not satisfy inclusion criteria.

Decode.TranslatedText: Findings About Events or Interventions Domain

EnumeratedItem.CodedValue: Administration site assessment
Blood Related History
Nasopharynx and Ocular Assessments
Vaccine Inoculation History
Assessment result

Decode.TranslatedText: Inclusion Exclusion Criteria Not Met Domain

EnumeratedItem.CodedValue: All values of vital signs, physical examination, laboratory tests, and 12-lead ECG are within allowed normal limits or not clinically significant. Results of pregnancy test are negative;
An employee of the Principal Investigator, study center, or Sponsor with direct involvement in the proposed study or other studies under the direction of that Principal Investigator or study center;
Any clinically significant result in the screening evaluations of Hepatitis B surface antigen, Hepatitis C antibody, human immunodeficiency virus antibody/antigen, and treponema pallidum antibody;
Any other situation which, in the opinion of the Investigator, would prevent the subject from fully participating in the study.
Any subject scheduled to undergo any surgical procedure, dental surgery, or hospitalization during the duration of the study;
Any subject who consumes more than 5 cigarettes per day within 3 months prior to screening, or who cannot stop using any tobacco- or nicotine-containing product during the duration of the trial;
Any subject who has an acute disease since last visit to the investigational site;
Any subject who has an acute or significant disease within 7 days prior to study drug administration or has a hospitalization within 1 month prior to study drug administration;
Any subject who has received any known hepatic enzyme inhibitor or inducer, or renal clearance (CLr) altering agents since last visit to the investigational site;
Any subject who has received any known hepatic enzyme inhibitor or inducer, or renal clearance (CLr) altering agents within 30 days prior to the first dose of study drug;
Any subject who is unwilling to stay in the clinical center for the required duration as per the protocol or to consume the standard meal to be provided as per the protocol;
Any subject who is unwilling to use reliable means of birth control within the coming year or any female subject who is pregnant or lactating;
Consumed more than 14 units of alcohol per week at any time within the 3 months before screening or reluctant to avoid alcohol intake during the duration of the trial;
Consumption of food or beverages containing glycetein within 24 hours prior to dosing, or consumption of grapefruit or grapefruit juice within 48 hours prior to dosing;
History of allergy or sensitivity to YJ001, any component of YJ001 for spray use, any component of placebo, aspirin, salicylic acid or drugs of the same class (eg, 5-aminosalicylic acid);
History of blood donation/bleeding of no less than 400 mL or receiving blood transfusion/using any blood product within 3 months prior to screening, except for bleeding during the menstruation;
History of vaccine inoculation within 4 weeks prior to screening;
History or evidence of clinically relevant pathology related to cardiovascular (CVS), central nervous system (CNS), respiratory tract, gastrointestinal (GI), endocrinology, immunology, hematology,
History or evidence of difficulty in donating blood;
Male or female, between the ages of 18 to 45 years old, both inclusive;
Participation in another investigational drug or device clinical trial within 3 months before dosing;
Positive screening on the alcohol (breathalyzer) test (> 0.0 mg/100 mL) or the urine drugs of abuse test (morphine, methamphetamine, ketamine, ecstasy, and marijuana);
Positive screening on the alcohol (breathalyzer) test or the urine drugs of abuse test (morphine, methamphetamine, ketamine, ecstasy, and marijuana);
Presence of erythema, edema, inflammation, damage or other condition at the application site that would affect drug absorption;
Presence of skin disorders, tattoos, or other skin conditions that may interfere with the objectives of the study (as per investigator evaluation);

Systemic or topical use of prescription drug since last visit to the investigational site, including nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, sucralfate or herbal preparations. Use of an over-the-counter
Systemic or topical use of prescription drug within 14 days before study drug dosing, including nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, sucralfate or herbal preparations. Use of an over-the-counter
The result of urine pregnancy test (women of childbearing potential) at screen was positive;
The skin condition of the subject’s feet scores > 2 on the dermal response scale;
The subjects can have a good communication with the investigator(s), and are able and willing to comply with all study requirements.
The subjects fully understand the objectives, procedures and potential adverse drug reactions of the trial, voluntarily consented for participation in the trial, and signed informed consent forms;
Weight: male >= 50.0 kg, female >= 45.0 kg; with calculated body mass index (BMI) between 19.0 and 26.0 kg/m2 (both inclusive), BMI = weight (kg) / height2 (m2);
Whether received vaccine inoculation or not since last visit to the investigational site.

Decode.TranslatedText: An employee of the Principal Investigator, study center, or Sponsor with direct involvement in the proposed study or other studies under the direction of that Principal Investigator or study center;
The skin condition of the subject’s feet scores > 2 on the dermal response scale;
Participation in another investigational drug or device clinical trial within 3 months before dosing;
History or evidence of clinically relevant pathology related to cardiovascular (CVS), central nervous system (CNS), respiratory tract, gastrointestinal (GI), endocrinology, immunology, hematology,
History of allergy or sensitivity to YJ001, any component of YJ001 for spray use, any component of placebo, aspirin, salicylic acid or drugs of the same class (eg, 5-aminosalicylic acid);
Presence of erythema, edema, inflammation, damage or other condition at the application site that would affect drug absorption;
Presence of skin disorders, tattoos, or other skin conditions that may interfere with the objectives of the study (as per investigator evaluation);
Consumed more than 14 units of alcohol per week at any time within the 3 months before screening or reluctant to avoid alcohol intake during the duration of the trial;
Any subject who consumes more than 5 cigarettes per day within 3 months prior to screening, or who cannot stop using any tobacco- or nicotine-containing product during the duration of the trial;
Positive screening on the alcohol (breathalyzer) test or the urine drugs of abuse test (morphine, methamphetamine, ketamine, ecstasy, and marijuana);
Any subject who is unwilling to use reliable means of birth control within the coming year or any female subject who is pregnant or lactating;
Systemic or topical use of prescription drug within 14 days before study drug dosing, including nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, sucralfate or herbal preparations. Use of an over-the-counter
Any subject who has received any known hepatic enzyme inhibitor or inducer, or renal clearance (CLr) altering agents within 30 days prior to the first dose of study drug;
Any clinically significant result in the screening evaluations of Hepatitis B surface antigen, Hepatitis C antibody, human immunodeficiency virus antibody/antigen, and treponema pallidum antibody;
Any subject who is unwilling to stay in the clinical center for the required duration as per the protocol or to consume the standard meal to be provided as per the protocol;
History of blood donation/bleeding of no less than 400 mL or receiving blood transfusion/using any blood product within 3 months prior to screening, except for bleeding during the menstruation;
Consumption of food or beverages containing glycetein within 24 hours prior to dosing, or consumption of grapefruit or grapefruit juice within 48 hours prior to dosing;
History or evidence of difficulty in donating blood;
Any subject who has an acute or significant disease within 7 days prior to study drug administration or has a hospitalization within 1 month prior to study drug administration;
Any subject scheduled to undergo any surgical procedure, dental surgery, or hospitalization during the duration of the study;
History of vaccine inoculation within 4 weeks prior to screening;
Any other situation which, in the opinion of the Investigator, would prevent the subject from fully participating in the study.
Positive screening on the alcohol (breathalyzer) test (> 0.0 mg/100 mL) or the urine drugs of abuse test (morphine, methamphetamine, ketamine, ecstasy, and marijuana);
The result of urine pregnancy test (women of childbearing potential) at screen was positive;
Any subject who has an acute disease since last visit to the investigational site;

Systemic or topical use of prescription drug since last visit to the investigational site, including nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, sucralfate or herbal preparations. Use of an over-the-counter
Any subject who has received any known hepatic enzyme inhibitor or inducer, or renal clearance (CLr) altering agents since last visit to the investigational site;
Whether received vaccine inoculation or not since last visit to the investigational site.
Male or female, between the ages of 18 to 45 years old, both inclusive;
Weight: male >= 50.0 kg, female >= 45.0 kg; with calculated body mass index (BMI) between 19.0 and 26.0 kg/m2 (both inclusive), BMI = weight (kg) / height2 (m2);
All values of vital signs, physical examination, laboratory tests, and 12-lead ECG are within allowed normal limits or not clinically significant. Results of pregnancy test are negative;
The subjects fully understand the objectives, procedures and potential adverse drug reactions of the trial, voluntarily consented for participation in the trial, and signed informed consent forms;
The subjects can have a good communication with the investigator(s), and are able and willing to comply with all study requirements.

Pregnancy Test
Serum Biochemistry
Urinary sediment
Urine Drugs of Abuse Test
Other reasons
Retest at screening
General Medical History
SPECIMEN PROPERTY
Lymph nodes

Decode.TranslatedText: Subject withdrew due to the AE
Whether with the scheduled sampling time
Trial Inclusion and Exclusion Criteria Domain

Early term
In clinic (D1)
In clinic (D2)
In clinic (D3)
In clinic (D4)

Screening (D-7 to D-1)

Admission to the clinic (D-2 to D-1) Unscheduled 1

EnumeratedItem.CodedValue: Other reasons
Retest at screening

Description.TranslatedText: Derived based on whether AESTDTC is on or after RFSTDTC
Last record with non-missing value prior to RFSTDTC
Y' for the last non-missing value prior to RFXSTDTC
--DY=(date portion of --DTC)-(date portion of RFSTDTC)+1 if --DTC is on or after RFSTDTC, --DY=(date portion of --DTC)-(date portion of RFSTDTC) if --DTC precedes RFSTDTC
Derived based on start-end dates defined in SE domain

MethodDef.Name: Algorithm to derive Reference Range Indicator

Description.TranslatedText: Indicate where a result falls with respect to reference range, derived for continuous measurements

External file: value of LLOQ from external files which are under D:\01Project\mo2856\CSR\01Source\03PKPD\PK If PC.PCTESTCD in ('YJ001', 'YJ001A', 'YJ001B') and PC.PCORRESU = 'ng/mL' then PCLLOQ = LLOQ multiply 1000; else set to LLOQ
Copied in character format from PCSTRESN. should store all numeric test results or findings
If PC.PCTESTCD in ('YJ001', 'YJ001A', 'YJ001B') and PC.PCORRESU = 'ng/mL' then PCSTRESN = numeric value of PC.PORRES multiply 1000; else set numeric value of PC.PCORRES
If PC.PCTESTCD in ('YJ001', 'YJ001A', 'YJ001B') then PCSTRESN = 'pg/mL'; else set to PC.PCORRESU
Date/time of last exposure to study treatment. Required for all randomized subjects, null for screen failures, unassigned or untreated subjects
the last date of participation for a subject for data included in submission
Date/time when subject was first exposed to study treatment, null for screen failures, unassigned or untreated subjects

MethodDef.Name: Algorithm to derive Date/Time of Reference Point

Description.TranslatedText: Date/time when subject was first exposed to study treatment, null for screen failures, unassigned or untreated subjects

when ETCD="SCRN": If subject discontinues then DM.RFPENDTC, else DM.RFSTDTC ; when ETCD in ("S1","S2","S3","S4","P"): If subject discontinues or no follow up records, then DM.RFPENDTC, else the first date from FT.FTDAT ; when ETCD="FU": DM.RFPENDTC ;
Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records within a dataset for a subject (or within a parameter, in the case of the Trial Summary domain)
DM.RFICDTC when ETCD="SCRN"; DM.RFSTDTC when ETCD in ("S1","S2","S3","S4","P"); The first date from FT.FTDAT when ETCD="FU";

MethodDef.Name: Algorithm to derive Reference Range in Std Units

Description.TranslatedText: Reference range for continuous measurements in standardized units, May multiplying the conversion-factor to transform the --ORRESU to --STRESU for the given test

MethodDef.Name: Algorithm to derive Character Result/Finding in Std Format

Description.TranslatedText: Copied or derived from --ORRES in a standard format or in standard units

MethodDef.Name: Algorithm to derive Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units

Description.TranslatedText: If --STRESC = "BLQ" then set 0, else copied in numeric format from --STRESC. should store all numeric test results or findings
Take the last visit date as SVENDTC per Subject per Visit.
Take distinct date per Subject per Visit from raw datasets with VISIT and use the first visit date as SVSTDTC. Do not keep the records if SVSTDTC and SVENDTC are equal to blank
Concatenation the FORM_NAME of Unplanned visit by "|"
=AE.AEACNO_DESC mapping to Controlled Terminology, if the value can not map to CT then set to "NOT APPLICABLE" and put the value into AEACNOTH
concatenate AE.AEENDAT with AE.AEENTIM, displayed in ISO 8601 format
=combine AE.AEOUT_DESC with AE.AESEQ mapping to CT
concatenate AE.AESTDAT with AE.AESTTIM, displayed in ISO 8601 format
assign: 'Prior Medications' for CMT; 'Concomitant Medications' for CM;
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.Standardized_Medication_Name with first 200 characters
Numerical dose part of CMT.cmtdstot; CM.cmdrout
Dose unit part of CMT.cmtdstot; CM.cmdrout mapping to CT
set to 'ONGOING' if CMT.cmongo_desc/CM.cmongo_desc='Yes'; else ' ' if CMT.cmongo_desc/CM.cmongo_desc in ('No','');
assign: 'Screening (D-7 to D-1)' for CMT; 'MEDICATION AT THE LAST VISIT' for CM;
Administration part of CMT.cmtdstot; CM.cmdrout mapping to CT
Sort by variable STUDYID, USUBJID, CMCAT, CMSPID, CMTRT, CMSTDTC, CMENDTC . Restart the numbering at each USUBJID with 1 (order sequentially).
set to 'SCRNFAIL' if RAND.RANDYN_DESC="No" else merge from external file: 盲底YJ整理-20210106发圣方.xlsx
Set 'Date of deviation' of external file
Set 'Description of the protocol violation/deviation' of external file less than 200 bytes
assign: 'Dose' for planned administration; 'Drug Residue' for drug residue;
outfile: '123456_药物残留浓度数据: YJ001-C'.Concentration
outfile: '123456_药物残留浓度数据: YJ001-C'.unit mapping to CT (umol/L)
Sort by variable STUDYID, USUBJID, ECTRT, ECSTDTC, ECENDTC. Restart the numbering at each USUBJID with 1 (order sequentially).
[ECG]: '12 LEAD UNSPECIFIED' [ECGM]: 'HOLTER CONTINUOUS ECG RECORDING' [ECGP]: '12 LEAD UNSPECIFIED'
set to 'NOT DONE' if missing EG.EGORRES
for scheduled visit refer to SDTM.TV for unschelduled visit, merge SDTM.SV by usubjid, date
assign: 240 480 720 960 - drug residue with formula: assigned.EXDOSE-EC.ECDOSE(where ECCAT='Drug Residue')*4/10^6*315.79*1000;
Sort by variable STUDYID, USUBJID, EXTRT, EXSTDTC, EXENDTC. Restart the numbering at each USUBJID with 1 (order sequentially).
[VH]: 'Vaccine Inoculation History' [BH]: 'Blood Related History' [EXV]: 'Administration site assessment' [NE]: 'Nasopharynx and Ocular Assessments'

[VH]: 'History of vaccine inoculation' [BH]: 'Blood Donation or Bleeding / Receiving Blood Transfusion / Using any Blood Product' if FATEST = 'Occurrence' BH.BHSTY_DESC if FATEST='Total volume' [EXV]: 'Administration Site' [NE]: 'Irritant to Nasopharynx and eye'
VH.VHYW_DESC set to BH.BHYW_DESC if FA.FATEST='Occurrence', else set to BH.BHTRM if FA.FATEST = Total volume' EXV.EXVRES NE.NEYN_DESC
set to "POINT" when FA,FAORRESU="Point(s); else FA.FAORRESU

[VH]: 'Occurrence' [BH]: 'Occurrence' for question 'Whether the subject ...' stack a 'Total volume' if the answer to question 'Whether the subject ...' is 'Yes' [EXV]: 'Assessment result' [NE]: 'Occurrence'
[VH]: 'OCCUR' [BH]: set to 'OCCUR' if FATEST='Occurrence' else set to 'VOL' if FATEST='Total volume' [EXV]: 'ASSRES' [NE]: 'OCCUR'
set to 'N' if IE_INC.ieyn_desc = "No" set to 'Y' if IE_EXC.ieyn_desc = "Yes" set to 'Y' if IE_EXM.ieyn_desc = "Yes"
LBH.LBORNRLO front numeric value of UP.upscope

set to 'Hepatitis B Virus Surface Antigen' if LBH.LBHTEST = 'Hepatitis B surface antigen' else set to 'Hepatitis C Virus Antibody' if LBH.LBHTEST = 'Hepatitis C virus antibody' else set to 'HIV Antigen and Antibody' if LBH.LBTEST='Human immunodificiency Virus antigen and antibody' else set to 'Treponema pallidum Antibody' if LBH.LBHTEST = 'Treponema pallidum antibody' else set to 'Hepatitis B Virus e Antibody' if UP.UPCHECK='Hepatitis B e antibody'

set to 'HBSAG' if LBH.LBHTEST = 'Hepatitis B surface antigen' else set to 'HCAB' if LBH.LBHTEST = 'Hepatitis C virus antibody' else set to 'HIVAGAB' if LBH.LBTEST='Human immunodificiency Virus antigen and antibody' else set to 'TPLAB' if LBH.LBHTEST = 'Treponema pallidum antibody' else set to 'HBEAB' if UP.UPCHECK='Hepatitis B e antibody'
LBH.LBORNRHI last numeric value of UP.upscope
for scheduled visit refer to SDTM.TV for unschelduled visit, merge SDTM.SV by usubjid, date
LBBR/LBBM/LBBRP/LBBC/LBCM/LBBCP/LBUR/LBUM/LBURP/ LBCT/LBCTP.lbdat, BHCG.BGDAT, UHCG.UGDAT, AC.ACDAT, DR.DRDAT, UP.UPDAT, LBBM/LBCM/LBUM.LBTIM
set to "Other unplanned examination" when from UP
set to 'NOT DONE' if missing of LB.LBORRES or BHCG.BGYN_DESC/UHCG.UGYN_DESC/UP.UPYN_DESC not equal "Yes".
if up.upcheck in "Hidden blood in urine","Leukocyte esterase","Others","Urine leukocyte esterase" then set to up.upscope;
For Unscheduled visit, merge from SV sheet by usubjid by date; For scheduled visit, set to scheduled visitnum;
For MH dataset: set to "General Medical History"; For AG dataset: set to "Allergy Medical History";
For MH dataset: set to MHONGO_DESC mapping to CT
For MH dataset: set to FOLDER_NAME
For MH dataset: set to MHSTDAT, displayed in ISO 8601 format
For MH dataset: set to MHTERM; For AG dataset: set to ALLERGY;
Set "SPECIMEN PROPERTY" when PCORRES is from S_IN.UC; else set "ANALYTE"
External file: value of LLOQ from external files which are under D:\01Project\mo2856\CSR\01Source\03PKPD\PK
UC.UCVOL External file: value of Concentration from external files which are under D:\01Project\mo2856\CSR\01Source\03PKPD\PK
Set "mL" when PCORRES is from S_IN.UC; else External file: value of unit from external files which are under D:\01Project\mo2856\CSR\01Source\03PKPD\PK
Set "URINE" when data is from 123456_尿样浓度数据_20190626 excel or S_IN.UC; else set "PLASMA"
Set "Volume" when PCORRES is from S_IN.UC; else External file: value of Analyte from external files which are under D:\01Project\mo2856\CSR\01Source\03PKPD\PK
Set Hour Nominal when data is from external files which are under D:\01Project\mo2856\CSR\01Source\03PKPD\PK when PCSPEC = “PLASMA”; else refer to following list
Set Hour Nominal when data is from external files which are under D:\01Project\mo2856\CSR\01Source\03PKPD\PK when PCSPEC=“PLASMA”; else refer to following list
Set 'Dose of D1' when PCRFTDTC is not missing
For Unscheduled visit, merge from SV sheet by usubjid by date; For scheduled visit, set to scheduled visitnum;
For Unscheduled visit, merge from SV sheet by usubjid by date; For scheduled visit, set to scheduled visitnum;
From the 3rd to the 36th row of "PKP"
"Screen" when ETCD="SCRN"; "YJ001 240mg" when ETCD="S1"; "YJ001 480mg" when ETCD="S2"; "YJ001 720mg" when ETCD="S3"; "YJ001 960mg" when ETCD="S4"; "Placebo" when ETCD="P"; "Follow-up" when ETCD="FU";
"SCREENING" when ETCD="SCRN"; "TREATMENT" when ETCD in ("S1","S2","S3","S4","P"); "FOLLOW-UP" when ETCD="FU";

if subject has any screening visit then output ETCD=“SCRN” for each subject; if DM.ARMCD='S1' and DM.RFSTDTC not missing then output ETCD=“S1” for each subject; if DM.ARMCD='S2' and DM.RFSTDTC not missing then output ETCD=“S2” for each subject; if DM.ARMCD='S3' and DM.RFSTDTC not missing then output ETCD=“S3” for each subject; if DM.ARMCD='S4' and DM.RFSTDTC not missing then output ETCD=“S4” for each subject; if DM.ARMCD='P' and DM.RFSTDTC not missing then output ETCD=“P” for each subject; if subject has any Follow-Up visit then output ETCD="FU" for each subject
Calculation of SEENDY = (Numeric version of SEENDTC - Numeric version of DM.RFSTDTC). Note: Add 1 if difference is >= 0.
Sort by variable STUDYID, USUBJID, TAETORD, ETCD, SESTDTC. Restart the numbering at each USUBJID with 1 (order sequentially).
Calculation of SESTDY = (Numeric version of SESTDTC - Numeric version of DM.RFSTDTC). Note: Add 1 if difference is >= 0.
1 when ETCD="SCRN"; 2 when ETCD in ("S1","S2","S3","S4","P"); 3 when ETCD="FU";
When stacked from SU, set SUCAT to 'Cigarettes', when stacked from SU1, set SUCAT to 'Alcohol'
For SU, set to SMOVOL dose frequency part (PER DAY). For SU1, set to ALCVOL dose frequency part (PER WEEK).
For SU, set to SMOVOL dose text part. For SU1, set to ALCVOL dose text part.
For SU, set to SMOVOL dose unit part (CIGARETTE). For SU1, set to ALCVOL dose unit part (UNIT).
For SU SU1, set to SUYW_DESC in NY format
Limited to 8 characters, Values often be the column name in the sponsor's operational dataset.
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.VAR13
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.VAR12
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.VAR11
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.VAR10
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.VAR9
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.VAR8
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.VAR7
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.VAR6
outfile: 'SAD 不良事件+合并用药编码-20210113: Concomitant Medication'.Drug_Code
List in CM Sheet when Dataset="SUPPCM"
List in CM Sheet when Dataset="SUPPCM"
List in CM Sheet when Dataset="SUPPCM"
List in CM Sheet when Dataset="SUPPCM"

剂量组别 from YJ001-健康人SAD-受试者基本情况表.xlsx, when "S1组（预）" then "S1 (Preliminary study)"; when "S2组（预）" then "S2 (Preliminary study)"; when "S1组" then "S1"; when "S2组" then "S2"; when "S3组" then "S3"; when "S4组" then "S4";
List in DM Sheet when Dataset="SUPPDM"
List in DM Sheet when Dataset="SUPPDM"
List in DM Sheet when Dataset="SUPPDM"
List in DM Sheet when Dataset="SUPPDM"
随机日期 from YJ001-健康人SAD-受试者基本情况表.xlsx
For DS dataset: set to DSOTHREA
Limited to 8 characters, Values often be the column name in the sponsor's operational dataset.
Set 'Measures' of external file over 200 bytes and less than 400 bytes
Set 'Measures' of external file less than 200 bytes
Set 'Description of the protocol violation/deviation' of external file over 200 bytes and less than 400 bytes
Set 'Description of the protocol violation/deviation' of external file over 400 bytes and less than 600 bytes
ECG.EGRES ECGP.EGRES ECGM.EGRES Note: first 200 char of text
ECG.EGRES ECGP.EGRES ECGM.EGRES Note: over 200 char of text
List in EG Sheet when Dataset="SUPPEG"
List in EG Sheet when Dataset="SUPPEG"
List in EG Sheet when Dataset="SUPPEG"
List in EG Sheet when Dataset="SUPPEG"
List in IS Sheet when Dataset="SUPPIS"
List in IS Sheet when Dataset="SUPPIS"
List in IS Sheet when Dataset="SUPPIS"
List in IS Sheet when Dataset="SUPPIS"
List in LB Sheet when Dataset="SUPPLB"
List in LB Sheet when Dataset="SUPPLB"
List in LB Sheet when Dataset="SUPPLB"
List in LB Sheet when Dataset="SUPPLB"
Limited to 8 characters, Values often be the column name in the sponsor's operational dataset.
List in PE Sheet when Dataset="SUPPPE"
List in PE Sheet when Dataset="SUPPPE"
List in PE Sheet when Dataset="SUPPPE"
List in PE Sheet when Dataset="SUPPPE"
List in VS Sheet when Dataset="SUPPVS"
List in VS Sheet when Dataset="SUPPVS"
List in VS Sheet when Dataset="SUPPVS"
List in VS Sheet when Dataset="SUPPVS"
Calculation of SVENDY = (Numeric version of SVENDTC - Numeric version of DM.RFSTDTC). Note: Add 1 if difference is >= 0.
Calculation of SVSTDY = (Numeric version of SVSTDTC - Numeric version of DM.RFSTDTC). Note: Add 1 if difference is >= 0.
Take all VISIT in raw datasets with visit. (Except: AE, BH, CM, CMT, DM, DS, MH, NDT, OP, UC, VH )

scheduled VISIT see the TV Sheet; For Unscheduled, VISIT and VISITNUM will be derived associated with scheduled VISIT. Sort all visit by date ,if date of unscheduled visit if after scheduled visit *, and before scheduled visit **, then map unscheduled visit to VISIT =visit * Unscheduled 1,and add 0.1 to VISITNUM of visit* as unscheduled visit's VISITNUM. If there are more than one unscheduled visit belongs to this visit interval ,then set VISIT like :visit* Unscheduled 1 ,visit* Unscheduled 2,.. ;set VISITNUM like: add 0.1,0.2 ... to vist* visitnum;
The code of the term in TSVAL, can be longer than 8 to accommodate the length of the external terminology
Name/version number of the Reference Terminology from which TSVALCD is taken
Name/version number of the Reference Terminology from which TSVALCD is taken
For Unscheduled visit, merge from SV sheet by usubjid by date; For scheduled visit, set to scheduled visitnum;
set to 'NOT DONE' if missing of VS.VSORRES
